Weeknight Sausage Pasta

A pantry-speed pasta built on browned Italian sausage and jarred sauce, dressed up with garlic, shallot, and a handful of optional finishers. Equally at home as a 25-minute weeknight dinner or, with the cream and fresh herbs added, something worth serving to guests.

Serves 4
Active Time ~15 minutes
Simmer 10 minutes
Total Time ~25 minutes
Photo coming soon

Ingredients

For the sauce

For the pasta

To finish (optional)

Method

1. Brown the Sausage

Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sausage and cook, breaking it into chunks as it browns, until deeply caramelized and cooked through, 6-8 minutes. Don't rush this step—the browning is most of the flavor. Drain excess fat, leaving about 1 tablespoon in the pan.

2. Cook the Aromatics

Add the shallots and cook for 4 minutes, until softened. Stir in the garlic and red pepper flakes, if using, and cook for 1 minute more, until fragrant.

3. Build the Sauce

Pour in the pasta sauce and stir to combine with the sausage and aromatics. Simmer on low for 9-10 minutes to let the flavors come together. Season to taste with salt and pepper, then stir in the cream or butter, if using, for extra richness.

4. Cook the Pasta

While the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta until al dente according to package directions. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.

5. Combine and Finish

Add the drained pasta to the sauce and toss gently to coat, loosening with reserved pasta water as needed. Top with Parmesan and fresh herbs, if using, and a squeeze of lemon juice for brightness.

Cook's Notes

Brown it properly Cook the sausage past the point of "done"—all the way to deeply caramelized. That's where the depth of flavor comes from, and jarred sauce needs the help.
Save the pasta water It's the easiest way to loosen and emulsify the sauce without watering down the flavor—always reserve a cup before draining.
Add herbs at the end Fresh basil or parsley added during cooking turns dull and bitter; stir them in right before serving to keep them bright.
Weeknight DinnerComfort Food
